companyNameJapanese: 株式会社アドバンテスト
description: World's #1 semiconductor test equipment (ATE) maker with ~50-58% overall global share, ~60-70% in memory test, and ~35-45% in SoC test. Forms a duopoly with Teradyne (~30%) controlling ~80% of the $6-7B+ global ATE market. Core platforms: V93000 EXA Scale (high-end SoC test for AI/HPC chips), T5500 (memory test: DRAM, NAND, HBM), T2000/AiR2X (analog/mixed-signal), T7038 SLT (system-level test). Called 'the ASML of the test industry.' Holds 100% share in advanced HBM memory testing requiring extended test times and multiple insertions per die. Revenue split: ~90% Test Systems, ~10% Services. FY ending March. FY2024 (Mar 2025): revenue 779.7B JPY (+60.3% YoY), OPM 29.3%, NI 161.2B JPY, ROE 37.8%. FY2025 guidance (revised up Jan 2026): revenue 1,070.0B JPY (+37.2%), OPM 42.4%, NI 328.5B JPY. Asia is 89.4% of revenue (primarily Taiwan, Korea, China). 4-for-1 stock split Oct 2023. Founded 1954 in Tokyo. ~5,800 employees. Manufacturing in Gunma (Japan) and Malaysia.

majorClients: Samsung Electronics (CMP-0018) — largest memory test customer; DRAM, NAND, HBM testers; also foundry SoC test
SK Hynix (CMP-0020) — major memory test customer; DRAM, HBM (dominant HBM tester supplier)
Micron Technology (CMP-0021) — major memory test customer; DRAM, NAND, HBM
TSMC ecosystem (CMP-0017) — SoC testers for foundry test operations; tests AI/HPC chips for NVIDIA, Apple, AMD, Qualcomm
Intel (CMP-0019) — SoC and legacy memory test
ASE Technology (CMP-0063) — OSAT final test operations (world #1 OSAT)
Amkor Technology (CMP-0064) — OSAT final test operations (world #2 OSAT)
indirectClients: NVIDIA (CMP-0028) — via TSMC/OSATs for GPU test (V93000 EXA Scale)
Apple — via TSMC for SoC test
AMD — via TSMC for CPU/GPU test
Qualcomm — via TSMC/Samsung for mobile SoC test
Broadcom — via TSMC for networking chip test
MediaTek — via TSMC for mobile SoC test
majorSuppliers: Sole-source component suppliers (not publicly disclosed; identified as key risk factor)
competitors: Teradyne Inc. (NASDAQ: TER) — world #2 ATE maker; ~30% overall share; gaining in HBM test; neck-and-neck in SoC test at 35-45% each
Cohu Inc. (NASDAQ: COHU) — distant #3; ~5% share; focused on power/RF testing and handlers
NI / Emerson (post-acquisition) — 2025 strategic collaboration with Advantest announced for AI ecosystems
Chinese domestic ATE (early stage, growing under localization pressure)
partners: Tokyo Seimitsu / ACCRETECH (CMP-0001) — joint development of die-level probers for AI/HPC testing, addressing Known Good Die requirements in 2.5D/3D packaging (announced Dec 2025)

FY2024_ended_Mar2025.note: All-time record results. Driven by explosive AI/HPC-related test demand. Gross margin expanded to 57.1% from 50.6%.

FY2025_9M_Apr_Dec2025.note: Record Q3 quarter: revenue 273.8B, OPM 41.5%. AI-related demand 'exceeded internal projections.'

FY2025_forecast_Mar2026.note: Guidance revised upward Jan 2026 (from 950B/374B OP). First-ever trillion-yen revenue year.

FY2023_ended_Mar2024.note: Cyclical downturn year after two years of 30%+ growth.

FY2026_consensus.note: Management anticipates 30-40% revenue growth; analyst consensus more conservative.

geographicRevenue_FY2024.chinaEstimate: ~10-20% (not separately disclosed; lower than peers due to customer HQ locations)

topShareholders: BlackRock, Inc.: increased stake by 5.2% in Q2 2023

dividendHistory.policy: Minimum 30 JPY/share annual. Target cumulative total return ratio 50%+ over 3 years (FY2024-FY2026) incl. buybacks.

shareBuybackHistory.Oct2025: Up to 150B JPY (~2.5% of shares outstanding)

capacityExpansion: Target: 5,000 ATE systems by end of FY2026, potentially 7,500 within two years
Manufacturing hub: Gunma Prefecture (Japan) — primary production facility
Significant capex to expand manufacturing capacity in line with AI-driven demand

productPortfolio.V93000_EXA_Scale: High-end SoC test for AI chips, HPC, automotive. Handles high current and high-speed serial interfaces. 25th anniversary in 2024.
productPortfolio.T5500_family: Memory test: DRAM, NAND, HBM. Core memory platform. Dominant in HBM test.
productPortfolio.T2000_AiR2X: Analog/mixed-signal, power IC testing. New air-cooled version launched Dec 2025.
productPortfolio.T7038_SLT: System-level test for complex AI/HPC workloads.

acquisitionHistory: 2011: Verigy — transformational; added SoC test leadership
2020: Essai Inc.; strategic alliance with PDF Solutions
keyRisks: Extreme valuation: PBR 30.4x, TTM P/E ~72x; priced for sustained high growth; any deceleration risks sharp correction
Customer concentration: Top 5 customers = 48% of FY2024 revenue (up from 29% in FY2023); memory makers dominate
Cyclicality: ATE is among the most cyclical sub-sectors; FY2023 showed -13.2% revenue decline after two years of 30%+ growth
AI demand sustainability: Revenue growth heavily linked to AI/HPC testing buildout; slowdown in AI capex would directly impact
Competition from Teradyne: Gaining ground in HBM test; SoC test is neck-and-neck at ~35-45% each
FX sensitivity: ~80% of sales in non-yen currencies (primarily USD); yen appreciation would compress margins
China/geopolitical: Asia is 89.4% of revenue; tightening export controls could restrict test equipment sales to China
Supply chain: Reliance on sole-source suppliers for certain components; manufacturing concentrated in Japan (natural disaster risk)
growthDrivers: AI/HPC testing: V93000 EXA Scale for GPU/accelerator test; SoC tester market estimated $8.5-9.5B for CY2026 (~30% YoY growth)
HBM demand: 60-70% memory test share; HBM requires 2-3x longer test times and multiple insertions vs standard DRAM
Advanced packaging: Die-level probers (JV with Tokyo Seimitsu) for Known Good Die testing in 2.5D/3D chiplet architectures
System-level test: T7038 SLT addresses growing need to test complex AI/HPC workloads at system level
Margin expansion: Q3 FY2025 gross margin reached 65.1% reflecting favorable AI product mix shift
Capacity expansion: Targeting 5,000-7,500 systems to meet multi-year AI demand wave
Buyback program: 150B JPY announced Oct 2025; 34.1M shares cancelled Feb 2026

Teradyne_TER.note: Main competitor; lower multiples reflecting lower market share and slower growth

valuationContext.note: Advantest trades at the highest P/B in global semiconductor equipment. Premium reflects monopoly-like memory test position, 60%+ revenue growth in FY2024, and central role in AI testing. 52-week range of 4,703-29,250 JPY (6.2x range) shows extreme volatility.

_externalRelationships.stockSplitNote: 4-for-1 stock split effective October 1, 2023. All per-share figures after this date are on post-split basis.

_metadata.dataSource: Advantest IR (advantest.com), Yahoo Finance, MarketScreener, StockAnalysis, Investing.com, CompaniesMarketCap, SimplyWallSt, Bloomberg, CNBC, TSPA Semiconductor, Seeking Alpha
_metadata.notes: Deep research update 2026-02-23. FY2024 record: revenue 779.7B (+60.3%), OPM 29.3%, NI 161.2B, ROE 37.8%. FY2025 guidance: revenue 1,070.0B (first trillion-yen year), OPM 42.4%. FY2025 9M: revenue 800.5B (+46.3%), Q3 gross margin 65.1%. Stock near all-time high 29,250 JPY (Jan 2026). P/E 56.3x, P/B 30.36x. Foreign ownership 45.23% (corrected from 50%). Top 5 customers 48% of revenue. 150B JPY buyback; 34.1M shares cancelled Feb 2026. Die-level prober JV with Tokyo Seimitsu (Dec 2025).
